mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in real activity demand, proxied by retail sales, raises expected corporate revenue growth and produces higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS April 2018 vintage value was 497559 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward 20-day Rank IC between 3-month RSAFS growth and S&P 500 forward returns is below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises rapidly, equity discount rates increase faster than near-term earnings expectations adjust, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.50 percentage points over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS April 2018 vintage value was 1.69 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with continued payroll expansion signals late-cycle labor tightness and rising wage pressure, leading to tighter policy expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 4.0 percent while PAYEMS rises over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE April 2018 vintage value was 3.9 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have a 60-day average S&P 500 return greater than the unconditional 60-day average or if the downside hit rate is below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals improving operating leverage and inventory demand for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day returns following the trigger are not higher than non-triggered periods at p < 0.10 or if the triggered mean return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction-linked spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10 percent over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST March 2018 vintage value was 1319 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of cases or if the triggered mean return exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices together with rising policy rates indicates inflation-driven monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above its 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS change has a non-negative coefficient in walk-forward 20-day return regressions in most rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor demand and increasing recession risk, leading investors to mark down earnings expectations and generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not have lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered instances or if the negative-return hit rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales growth combined with accelerating consumer prices signals a squeeze on real consumption, lowering expected earnings for cyclical firms and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is below zero while CPIAUCSL 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a 60-day S&P 500 mean return above zero and no statistically significant underperformance versus the unconditional sample at p < 0.10.
